title: Bitter Tender Portuguese Shrimp Flatbread
description: A vibrant, lowcarb flatbread featuring tender shrimp, a zesty Portuguese tadka, and crisp phyllo, finished with a hint of bitterness from lemon zest and chili.
instructions: Grease a small baking tin or line with parchment paper.
Lay the phyllo sheets on top of each other, brushing each sheet lightly with olive oil and sprinkling a pinch of salt; bake for 8–10 minutes until golden and crisp, then set aside to cool.

Meanwhile, in a small skillet, heat the butter over medium heat; add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, dried chili flakes, and curry leaves, letting them sizzle for 30 seconds until fragrant; add minced garlic and red chili flakes, cooking for another minute.
Transfer the shrimp to the skillet, season with salt and pepper, and sauté for 2–3 minutes on each side until pink and opaque; squeeze lemon juice over the shrimp and stir in lemon zest and cilantro.
Remove the shrimp from heat and let rest for 1 minute.
Place the cooled phyllo flatbread on a serving platter; top with the shrimp mixture, arranging shrimp in a single layer for an elegant presentation.
Drizzle a little extra olive oil over the top and serve immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
